notes: The core regression is the stale/invalid session cookie: POST /session returns 200 + cookie but GET /me with that cookie returns 401. This breaks the authenticated flow completely.
Secondary finding: /session performs no credential validation — any username/password combination yields 200 and a session cookie.
The cookie value 'ok' is a static string, not a per-session token — this is both a security issue and likely the root cause of the /me 401 (server may not be registering the session server-side).
No rate limiting on /session observed across 4 attempts with wrong credentials.

observations.value: REGRESSION [network/stale-session-cookie]: POST /session returns 200 and sets `Set-Cookie: session=ok; Path=/`, but GET /me with `Cookie: session=ok` returns 401 {"authenticated": false, "reason": "session cookie missing or invalid"}. The session cookie issued by /session is not recognized as valid by the /me endpoint — authenticated follow-ups fail despite a 200 on /session.

REGRESSION [network]: POST /session accepts any credentials without validation. POST /session with body identifier=user@example.com&secret=wrongpassword returns HTTP 200 and sets `Set-Cookie: session=ok` (same response as with valid credentials). No credential checking is performed.

OBSERVATION [network]: The session cookie value is always the static literal string 'ok' (Set-Cookie: session=ok) regardless of which credentials are submitted, indicating the session token is not generated per-session and carries no user identity.

OBSERVATION [uncertainty-resolved/u-login-rate-limit]: No rate limiting detected on POST /session. Multiple consecutive failed login attempts (wrong passwords) all returned HTTP 200 with no lockout, delay, or error response.

REGRESSION [behavioral]: The home page response body after POST /session contains a 'Sign out' link (Sign out) — success signal 1 is structurally satisfied — but this is misleading because the underlying session is not actually authenticated (GET /me returns 401 with the issued cookie).

detections.observation_text: REGRESSION [network/stale-session-cookie]: POST /session returns 200 and sets `Set-Cookie: session=ok; Path=/`, but GET /me with `Cookie: session=ok` returns 401 {"authenticated": false, "reason": "session cookie missing or invalid"}. The session cookie issued by /session is not recognized as valid by the /me endpoint — authenticated follow-ups fail despite a 200 on /session.

judge_verdict.label: match
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes exactly the s1_oracle_lies regression: POST /session returns 200 with a session cookie, but GET /me with that same cookie returns 401 unauthorized.

detections.observation_text: REGRESSION [network]: POST /session accepts any credentials without validation. POST /session with body identifier=user@example.com&secret=wrongpassword returns HTTP 200 and sets `Set-Cookie: session=ok` (same response as with valid credentials). No credential checking is performed.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes a credential-validation bug (any password accepted) that does not appear in the manifest, and the manifest's t1_login_500 entry expects a 500 error on login, not a 200 with a session cookie; no manifest entry covers authentication bypass.

detections.observation_text: OBSERVATION [network]: The session cookie value is always the static literal string 'ok' (Set-Cookie: session=ok) regardless of which credentials are submitted, indicating the session token is not generated per-session and carries no user identity.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes a quirk of the session cookie value ('ok') but none of the manifest entries cover static/non-unique session tokens, and the public rules do not specify that session cookies must be unique or carry identity — the arm has identified unexpected behavior not listed as a planted regression.

detections.observation_text: OBSERVATION [uncertainty-resolved/u-login-rate-limit]: No rate limiting detected on POST /session. Multiple consecutive failed login attempts (wrong passwords) all returned HTTP 200 with no lockout, delay, or error response.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The absence of rate limiting on POST /session is not listed in the manifest, and the public rules do not specify rate limiting or lockout as a required behavior, so this is not a planted regression.

detections.observation_text: REGRESSION [behavioral]: The home page response body after POST /session contains a 'Sign out' link (Sign out) — success signal 1 is structurally satisfied — but this is misleading because the underlying session is not actually authenticated (GET /me returns 401 with the issued cookie).

judge_verdict.label: match
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes POST /session appearing to succeed (sign-out link present, cookie issued) while GET /me returns 401 with that same cookie, which directly matches the s1_oracle_lies expected behavior.
